differential_diagnoses: Rickettsia rickettsii infection (Rocky Mountain spotted fever)
next_steps: Perform serum and urine Leptospira PCR and MAT (Microscopic Agglutination Test)
Send blood smears for Babesia and Anaplasma organisms
Obtain serial bedside thoracic ultrasound and monitor arterial blood gases
Consult infectious disease, nephrology, and potentially hepatology for consideration of CRRT/hemodialysis
Perform bronchoalveolar lavage if pulmonary status continues to decline
Perform serum and urine PCR for Leptospira and serology (MAT)
Perform diagnostic bronchoscopy with bronchoalveolar lavage to assess for diffuse alveolar hemorrhage
Send peripheral blood smear for Babesia and Anaplasma and evaluate for hemolysis
Consult nephrology for immediate evaluation for CRRT due to worsening renal failure and potential volume overload
Obtain hantavirus-specific serology and PCR testing
Perform echocardiogram to evaluate cardiac function and assess for fluid overload versus pulmonary edema
Initiate and continue targeted antibiotic therapy for suspected severe leptospirosis
